# API & Cache Requirements Checklist: Remote Localization V1

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality for runtime remote localization APIs, cache behavior, failure recovery, merge rules, and V1 scope boundaries.
**Created**: 2026-07-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the required capabilities of the consumer-provided remote connector fully specified for both global and per-locale flows? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how connector support or non-support is represented before a remote operation starts? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK003 Are cache read, cache write, cache preservation, and cache fallback requirements documented as separate outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for applying cached remote data during normal locale loading as distinct from downloading new remote data? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK005 Are active-locale reload and listener notification requirements complete for successful remote updates that affect the current dictionary? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK006 Are the out-of-scope V1 workflows explicitly listed and separated from runtime pull requirements? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "structured success or failure result" defined with explicit statuses, fields, and failure-code expectations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "normalized localization results" clarified enough to distinguish connector-owned backend mapping from package-owned validation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK009 Are "all changed locale payloads" and "only the requested locale payload" precise enough to prevent ambiguous partial-update behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the remote version comparison rule stated with enough precision for equal, older, missing, and strictly newer versions? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK011 Is the 10-second timeout, one retry, and 2-second backoff requirement unambiguous about whether it applies to check operations, download operations, or both? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK012 Is duplicate in-flight request skipping defined with a clear identity for global scope and per-locale scope? [Clarity, Spec §FR-020]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK013 Do startup local-first requirements align with cache application and active-locale reload requirements without implying startup blocking? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK014 Do no-update requirements align with cache preservation requirements so unchanged remote versions do not cause unnecessary downloads or cache replacement? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK015 Are global and per-locale unsupported-mode requirements consistent across acceptance scenarios, functional requirements, and planned result statuses? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK016 Do merge precedence requirements align with protected-key requirements and the default `override: true` behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016]
- [ ] CHK017 Are logging and metrics requirements consistent with secret-handling requirements and sanitized failure expectations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-023]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK018 Can each remote update outcome be objectively assessed from the documented success criteria without relying on implementation-specific class names? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK019 Are cache recovery success criteria measurable for both "last valid remote cache remains usable" and "local assets remain usable when no valid cache exists"? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK020 Is the protected-key success criterion measurable for nested values, metadata wrappers, and missing override metadata? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-007, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-017]
- [ ] CHK021 Is the no-asset-mutation success criterion broad enough to cover runtime pull, cache writes, and excluded local asset update workflows? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-008, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK022 Is the no-secret success criterion measurable across cache data, cache keys, connector errors, logs, and metrics? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-009, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-023]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 Are primary global update scenarios covered for no update, update available, unsupported mode, and failure outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK024 Are primary per-locale update scenarios covered for update available, requested-locale mismatch, unsupported mode, stale payload, and failure outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK025 Are recovery requirements defined for check failure, download failure, payload parsing failure, corrupt cache, stale cache, and incomplete cache? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK026 Are concurrency requirements defined for queued requests, duplicate in-flight requests, failure propagation, and result ordering? [Coverage, Spec §FR-020, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK027 Are runtime startup scenarios covered for no remote configuration, configured remote with startup check disabled, and configured remote with startup check enabled? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK028 Are requirements defined for remote payloads that exceed expected volume assumptions or contain more locales than the documented scale target? [Gap, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK029 Are fallback requirements defined for platforms where persistent file-based cache storage is unavailable or read-only? [Gap, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK030 Are requirements defined for pruning oldest cache entries on write failure, including selection criteria and preservation guarantees? [Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK031 Are requirements defined for secret-like values embedded inside connector failure messages or backend-provided payload metadata? [Gap, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK032 Are requirements defined for remote payload keys that conflict with override metadata marker keys? [Gap, Spec §FR-017]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK033 Are assumptions about UTC timestamp interpretation and strict newer-than comparison traceable to version entity requirements and contract expectations? [Traceability, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Phase 1 Design]
- [ ] CHK034 Are dependencies on existing parser normalization documented enough to cover JSON, ARB, YAML, CSV, nested values, plural/select, and data-type patterns? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Dependencies]
- [ ] CHK035 Is responsibility split clearly documented between consumer-owned authentication/backend details and package-owned cache, merge, and result handling? [Dependency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Summary]
- [ ] CHK036 Are platform cache-location dependencies documented with an owner or decision path before implementation tasks add or avoid new dependencies? [Dependency, Plan §Dependencies, Tasks §Phase 1]
- [ ] CHK037 Are V1 boundary assumptions about CLI push, Catalog sync, remote history, and local asset-file updates reflected consistently in spec, plan, and tasks? [Assumption, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Scale/Scope, Tasks §Notes]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK038 Is it clear whether metrics are part of the public API contract or an internal extension point? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK039 Is it clear whether startup remote failures return structured results to callers or are only logged because startup work is non-blocking? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK040 Is the relationship between "remote cache" and "in-memory fallback" defined well enough to avoid conflicting persistence expectations? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK041 Are public API names intentionally deferred while still leaving enough contract detail for implementation and tests? [Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Project Structure]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
